seoTitle: Stepney vs Stewart Range — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stepney and Stewart Range. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stepney vs Stewart Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stepney and Stewart Range.
heading: Stepney vs Stewart Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stepney, South Australia and Stewart Range, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stepney has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Stewart Range (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stepney or Stewart Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stepney has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Stewart Range scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stepney scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Stewart Range scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stepney or Stewart Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stepney has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
